appearance: Impeccably groomed Victorian scholar in tailored dove-gray traveling suits and high-collared blouses trimmed with subtle lace. Keeps raven hair swept into a precise chignon, gloves always on, posture ramrod straight. Eyes are cold blue behind gilt spectacles, and every motion is measured—quills, satchels, and warding implements arranged with obsessive symmetry.
biography: Dr. Margaret Ashford was Embraced in 1880s Philadelphia after earning a doctorate that polite society never expected a woman to attain. A prodigy of protective Thaumaturgy, she served the East Coast chantries for a century before the Pyramid reassigned her to Phoenix three months ago to study indigenous warding. To her, the desert posting is exile, yet she refuses to let resentment dull her scholarship. She maintains strict Victorian decorum, documents every experiment in leather-bound journals, and quietly manipulates the chantry’s defenses while corresponding with her Philadelphia sire. Unbeknownst to her, a fae enchantment lingers through her beloved horse Aonbharr, subtly twisting her dreams even as she labors to make the Phoenix haven impregnable.
notes: Tremere protective-magic savant newly stationed in Phoenix. Resents the assignment but remains loyal to the Pyramid; will bargain knowledge for assistance with warding projects. Underlying fae enchantment via her horse can become a story hook. Ideal ST contact for ritual research, defensive wards, or political grudges inside the Tremere hierarchy.
equipment: Research materials, Victorian-era journals, occult texts on ward-craft

merits_flaws.name: Enchanted (Aonbharr)
merits_flaws.description: Under fae enchantment via her horse, unknown to her
